title: coulteria pumila
intro: Coulteria pumila, also known as Coulter's cotton rose or Coulter's rose, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western United States and Canada. The medicinal properties of Coulteria pumila include its use as an anti-inflammatory agent, and it has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Coulteria pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Coulteria pumila can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
